Tests JSON stringification of specific character operations resulting in "null".

# Purpose
This code is a short script that tests the behavior of the `JSON.stringify` function when applied to certain expressions involving the Unicode character '�'. It assigns the result of `JSON.stringify` to variables `a`, `b`, and `c`, each time using a different expression that coerces the character '�' into a number. The script then uses `assert` statements to verify that the result of each `JSON.stringify` call is the string `"null"`. This indicates that the expressions evaluate to `NaN`, which `JSON.stringify` converts to `null`. The script serves as a test to confirm the handling of invalid number conversions in JSON serialization.
